name: EGO Power+ LB6504 56V 650 CFM Cordless Leaf Blower

specs.Battery: 2.5Ah minimum (check kit — battery may or may not be included)
specs.Ecosystem: EGO 56V (shares with LM2156SP mower, trimmers, etc.)

whatWorks: 650 CFM at 180 MPH is gas-blower territory — enough to move heavy wet leaf piles, clear gravel driveways, and push debris off larger areas in a single pass. Most cordless blowers at this price cap out at 400–500 CFM.

The EGO 56V battery ecosystem means your mower, trimmer, and blower all share the same batteries and charger. This is the EGO ecosystem's primary selling point — and it delivers real convenience for owners who make the ecosystem commitment.
Variable speed trigger plus a turbo boost button gives fine control. Low speed for blowing around garden beds without disturbing mulch; turbo for packed leaf piles and wet debris.
Weather-sealed construction handles Canadian spring and fall conditions — light rain or morning dew won't interrupt a yard cleanup session.
worthKnowing: This is a high-power blower and it shows in the weight: ~7.5 lb without battery, heavier with. Extended use (30+ minutes) fatigues arms more than lighter 400 CFM alternatives.
Battery inclusion varies by listing — the tool-only listing is common and requires purchasing a 56V EGO battery separately (2.5Ah minimum; 5Ah or 7.5Ah for best runtime).
Turbo mode at 650 CFM is genuinely loud — comparable to a gas blower in noise level. The 'quieter than gas' advantage is reduced significantly in turbo mode.
Not rated for roof or gutter blowing — the blower tube direction makes it awkward and the high CFM makes it difficult to control debris on elevated surfaces.

commonComplaints.title: Heavy after 30 minutes of sustained…
commonComplaints.body: Heavy after 30 minutes of sustained use — 7.5 pounds doesn't sound like much but holding it at arm angle while blowing is tiring. My old gas blower had a harness.

commonComplaints.title: Turbo mode drains the battery fast…
commonComplaints.body: Turbo mode drains the battery fast. At full power I get maybe 20–25 minutes on the 2.5Ah battery. Get the 5Ah if you have a big property.

commonComplaints.title: Battery not included in base listing…
commonComplaints.body: Battery not included in base listing — make sure you order the kit version or already have an EGO 56V battery. Very easy to miss.

commonComplaints.title: Still noisy on turbo — neighbours…
commonComplaints.body: Still noisy on turbo — neighbours are not as forgiving as I expected. On low speed it's fine but turbo is genuinely loud.

verdict: The EGO LB6504 delivers gas-equivalent blowing power in a cordless package — 650 CFM is genuinely impressive and handles wet autumn leaves, gravel driveways, and large Canadian yards without hesitation. The weight and noise on turbo mode are real tradeoffs worth knowing, and battery inclusion in the listing needs confirming before purchase. For EGO 56V ecosystem users, it's the natural blower addition. For buyers starting fresh, it's the best-performing cordless leaf blower under $300 CAD.

realBuyers.quote: Cleared my entire 0.4-acre property of fall leaves on one charge. Gas blower couldn't have done it faster.
Same battery as my EGO mower and trimmer. I swap batteries between tools all afternoon without thinking about it.
As powerful as my neighbour's gas blower. He was pretty surprised when I told him it was battery.
On low speed it's genuinely quiet. I can actually use it Sunday morning without any guilt.

sources.title: Best Cordless Leaf Blowers 2025 — Popular Mechanics
EGO Leaf Blower Review — The Spruce

metaDescription: EGO Power+ LB6504 56V 650 CFM cordless leaf blower review for Canada: gas-equivalent airflow, EGO 56V ecosystem, variable speed. Honest on weight and turbo noise. ~$229 CAD.
introText: The EGO Power+ LB6504 is a 56V cordless leaf blower with 650 CFM output — matching or exceeding most gas blowers while running on the same battery as EGO's mowers and trimmers. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B0832FZT4V) at around $229 CAD.
